steps.title: Find the slope of line r
steps.content:
Rewrite $6x - 5y = 9$ in slope-intercept form:
$$-5y = -6x + 9 \implies y = \frac{6}{5}x - \frac{9}{5}$$
Slope of line $r = \dfrac{6}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines slope rule
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes, so the slope of line $s$ equals the slope of line $r = \dfrac{6}{5}$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-6$: Slope $-6 e rac{6}{5}$. A line with slope $-6$ is not parallel to $r$. ✗
  • Choice B — $-5$: Slope $-5 e rac{6}{5}$. Not parallel. ✗
  • Choice C — $ rac{5}{6}$: Slope $ rac{5}{6} e rac{6}{5}$. This is the reciprocal, not the slope. ✗
  • Choice D — $ rac{6}{5}$: Slope $ rac{6}{5}$ matches the slope of line $r$ exactly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
